texto: If we are unable to tolerate ourselves when we are alone, how can we expect anyone else to be enriched by our company? Before we can have a solid relationship with another, we must have a relationship with ourselves. We are challenged to learn to listen to ourselves. We have to be able to stand alone before we can truly stand beside another.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Gerald Corey que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “If we are unable to tolerate ourselves when we are alone, how can we expect anyone else to be enr…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, existentialism, ourselves, com foco em reflexao.
